channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Opinion: A well-deserved statue for a hero rat
item.description: Cambodia is recognizing the life-saving contributions of a rat named Magawa with a statue. The late rat sniffed out landmines for a non-profit group, and in a short career helped find more than 100.
item.content:encoded: Cambodia is recognizing the life-saving contributions of a rat named Magawa with a statue. The late rat sniffed out landmines for a non-profit group, and in a short career helped find more than 100.
item.dc:creator: Scott Simon

item.title: The Great Green Wall's one of the world's most ambitious eco-projects. Is it working?
item.description: It's a global effort with a multibillion dollar price tag. Among its aims: re-greening nearly 250 million acres, planting 4,000 miles of trees, helping farmers, creating jobs, sequestering carbon.
item.content:encoded:
It's a global effort with a multibillion dollar price tag. Among its aims: re-greening nearly 250 million acres, planting 4,000 miles of trees, helping farmers, creating jobs, sequestering carbon.

(Image credit: Tommy Trenchard for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Julie Bourdin

item.title: Gut troubles? This gastroenterologist has tips to help you achieve 'poophoria'
item.description: In her new book You've Been Pooping All Wrong, Dr. Trisha Pasricha shares habits and practices to make your relationship with your solid waste as smooth as possible
item.content:encoded: In her new book You've Been Pooping All Wrong, Dr. Trisha Pasricha shares habits and practices to make your relationship with your solid waste as smooth as possible
item.dc:creator: Andrea Muraskin

item.title: India cracks down on satirists for turning its prime minister into a punch line
item.description: India's satirists are turning Prime Minister Narendra Modi into a punch line — and the government is hitting back.
item.content:encoded:
India's satirists are turning Prime Minister Narendra Modi into a punch line — and the government is hitting back.

(Image credit: Ludovic Marin)
item.dc:creator: Omkar Khandekar

item.title: After a whirlwind mission to the moon, astronauts are back home. Here's what's next
item.description: The Orion crew module containing the four Artemis II astronauts splashed down in the Pacific Ocean Friday evening.
item.content:encoded:
The Orion crew module containing the four Artemis II astronauts splashed down in the Pacific Ocean Friday evening.

(Image credit: Bill Ingalls)
item.dc:creator: Brendan Byrne

item.title: In Syria, instability allowed ISIS fighters to flee camps, but many of their families still detained
item.description: NPR visits the last detention camp for ISIS wives and children in an increasingly precarious northeastern Syria.
item.content:encoded:
NPR visits the last detention camp for ISIS wives and children in an increasingly precarious northeastern Syria.

(Image credit: Claire Harbage)
item.dc:creator: Jane Arraf

item.title: How AI is getting better at finding security holes
item.description: Anthropic announced this week that its new model found security flaws in "every major operating system and web browser." Even before the news, AI models had gotten dramatically better at finding bugs.
item.content:encoded:
Anthropic announced this week that its new model found security flaws in "every major operating system and web browser." Even before the news, AI models had gotten dramatically better at finding bugs.

(Image credit: Patrick Sison)
item.dc:creator: Huo Jingnan

item.title: Peru's election: A battle for the Presidency amid political chaos and crime
item.description: With 35 candidates in the race, Peru is set to elect its 9th president in less than a decade. Amid rising corruption and crime, voters are left asking: Can this election finally break the cycle?
item.content:encoded:
With 35 candidates in the race, Peru is set to elect its 9th president in less than a decade. Amid rising corruption and crime, voters are left asking: Can this election finally break the cycle?

(Image credit: Martin Mejia)
item.dc:creator: Simeon Tegel

item.title: Pakistan hosts U.S.-Iran peace talks after weeks of frantic diplomacy
item.description: Pakistan's capital, Islamabad, is set to host peace talks today with leaders from Iran and the US, including Vice President J.D. Vance.
item.content:encoded:
Pakistan's capital, Islamabad, is set to host peace talks today with leaders from Iran and the US, including Vice President J.D. Vance.

(Image credit: Anjum Naveed)
item.dc:creator: Betsy Joles

item.title: WATCH: Artemis II astronauts splash down on Earth
item.description: The four astronauts aboard NASA's Artemis II mission splashed down on Earth, after a successful visit to the moon.
item.content:encoded: The four astronauts aboard NASA's Artemis II mission splashed down on Earth, after a successful visit to the moon.
item.dc:creator: Amina Khan
